title: CLI-Anything: Agent-Native CLI Framework
description: A guided tour of the CLI-Anything framework — how it transforms GUI applications into AI-agent-controllable CLIs using a proven 7-phase methodology, with 11 reference implementations and multi-platform support.

steps.title: Welcome to CLI-Anything
steps.description: cli-anything is a framework for transforming GUI applications into agent-native CLI tools. This codebase contains the core Claude Code plugin, integrations for 4 AI platforms, and 11 fully tested reference harnesses spanning image editing, 3D modeling, audio, video, office, streaming, and more — with over 1500 passing tests at a 100% pass rate.

steps.title: The Core Plugin
steps.description: The cli-anything-plugin directory is the heart of the project. It contains the Claude Code plugin that implements the 7-phase harness generation methodology, including HARNESS.md (the source-of-truth SOP), QUICKSTART.md for getting started, and the repl_skin.py shared REPL component that provides a unified interactive interface across all generated CLIs.

steps.title: Plugin Command Definitions
steps.description: The commands directory contains markdown-based command definitions that power the Claude Code plugin. Each file defines a slash command: cli-anything (main generation), refine (iterate on harness), test (run tests), validate (check harness quality), and list (show available harnesses). These commands orchestrate the 7-phase methodology.

steps.title: Reference Harness: GIMP
steps.description: Each of the 11 software harnesses follows an identical structure generated by the plugin. Here's the GIMP harness as an example: a Click-based Python CLI with core modules for project management, session handling (undo/redo), and export — plus comprehensive tests. Every harness lives under a cli-anything namespace package for consistent pip installation.

steps.title: 11 Reference Implementations
steps.description: The framework has been battle-tested across 11 professional applications: GIMP & Inkscape (image editing), Blender (3D), Audacity (audio), Kdenlive & Shotcut (video), LibreOffice (office), OBS Studio (streaming), Zoom (conferencing), Draw.io (diagramming), and AnyGen (AI generation). Each follows the same proven structure, demonstrating the methodology's versatility.

steps.title: Multi-Platform Support
steps.description: Beyond Claude Code, CLI-Anything supports multiple AI agent platforms. The codex-skill directory provides OpenAI Codex integration with install scripts and agent YAML configs. opencode-commands offers OpenCode support via markdown command definitions. qoder-plugin enables Qodercli integration through a registration script. Each platform adapter translates the same core methodology into platform-native formats.

steps.title: Distribution & Marketplace
steps.description: The claude-plugin directory contains marketplace configuration for distributing the plugin through the Claude Code marketplace. Each generated harness also includes a setup.py for PyPI publishing, enabling pip install of any agent harness. The PUBLISHING.md guide in the plugin directory covers the full distribution workflow.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: You've now seen the complete CLI-Anything ecosystem: the core plugin with its 7-phase methodology, 5 slash commands, 11 proven reference implementations, and integrations for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Qodercli. To get started, check out QUICKSTART.md for a 5-minute walkthrough, or dive into HARNESS.md for the full methodology specification.
